message:
I am thinking...: The advisory has returned its output. Now I need to:
1. Note that the exact advisory name "MCU Factory Spec Advisory" was NOT found — the closest match is "Spec Advisory" (id: 4b20feaa-fec6-4260-a356-871298ba6d20). I ran it and got a response.
2. Save the raw advisory output to docs/advisory/spec_advisory.md
3. Create link_IPs.json and run ipm_linker.py
4. Return structured status report

Let me also note the name discrepancy clearly in the advisory output file and in my return.

The advisory output is the "Golden Micro-Architecture Specification" that was returned. I need to save this verbatim.

For the IP list, based on the peripherals:
- NC_GPIO (3 instances)
- NC_UART (4 instances)
- NC_SPI (2 instances)
- NC_I2C (3 instances)
- NC_TMR (6 instances)
- NC_PWM (2 instances)
- NC_DMAC (1 instance)
- NC_CRC (1 instance)
- NC_AES (1 instance)
- NC_WWDT (1 instance)
- NC_RTC (1 instance)
- NC_SYSTICK (1 instance)
- NC_XIP (flash controller)
- NC_AMBA (bus infrastructure)
- NC_RV32 (CPU / hazard3)
- NC_COMMON (utilities)
- CF_SRAM_1024x32 (SRAM macro - required)

The link_IPs.json should have unique IP names (not instances, just the IP package names).
